methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $63M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 23 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 45.3x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Open Cosmos, a Growth-stage space and aerospace company founded by Rafel Jordà Siquier, has raised $63M to date. The valuation landscape includes 5 public Space & Aerospace comparables (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, and Blue Origin, though a narrow peer cohort at identical stage and valuation tier is limited.

Of three triangulation methods examined, only Stage-Progression proved applicable. This method uses the $63M total raised as a valuation proxy and applies a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement, yielding $76M. The Peer-Median approach was not viable due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the Space & Aerospace sector—while 23 same-sector peers exist broadly, none of the narrow filtering criteria yielded three or more datapoints. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be applied because the target's revenue is not disclosed; the sector P/S median of 45.3x across six public tickers remains unusable without this metric.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $64M (low) to $87M (high), with a median point of $76M. This range reflects a single applicable method and carries meaningful uncertainty stemming from limited peer granularity and the absence of revenue data. Key variables affecting valuation precision include customer concentration, near-term revenue visibility, and capital efficiency relative to private-sector peers in the Space & Aerospace sector.
